args.memo: Accrual for Larkfield Instruments invoice LK-7021, dated 2026-06-13

proposed.reasoning: The invoice is structurally valid (all R1–R4 checks passed), originates from a new vendor with no prior history (so no duplicate or variance concerns), and contains all required fields including tax_id and vendor_ref. Per policy, clean invoices from new vendors trigger accrual via journal entry before payment can be scheduled.

trace.observation: No prior invoices recalled for Larkfield Instruments — this vendor is new (first time seen).
trace.reasoning: I must first recall Larkfield Instruments' vendor history to determine if they are a known, recurring vendor and to establish baseline data for duplicate and variance checks.

trace.observation: Structural validation R1–R4 all pass; the invoice is well-formed.
trace.reasoning: Since the vendor is new (no prior history), the next step is to validate the invoice's structural integrity — checking for required fields, tax reconciliation, and line-item consistency — before deciding on a terminal action.

trace.observation: No duplicate of a previously processed invoice was found.
trace.reasoning: Although the vendor is new, duplicate detection (R5) still applies: same vendor + reference or same vendor + amount + date could indicate a duplicate even on first encounter — so we must rule it out before proceeding.

trace.observation: New vendor — no prior amount history to compare against.
trace.reasoning: Although the vendor is new (no prior invoices), compute_variance_vs_history is still safe to call — it will return 'no history' rather than an error, and confirms definitively that no historical baseline exists for variance analysis. This closes the R6 check and completes the evidence set.
